description:
You are the site administrator of a small web-service.  
 All registered accounts are listed in the TAB-separated file  

   /home/user/site/accounts.tsv  

 Each line of this file has exactly four columns in the following order:

   1. numeric user_id  
   2. username (lower-case, no spaces)  
   3. e-mail address  
   4. textual role name  

 The file currently contains these four records (TAB characters separate the fields, there is **no** header row):

   101<TAB>alice<TAB>alice@example.com<TAB>admin  
   102<TAB>bob<TAB>bob@domain.com<TAB>editor  
   103<TAB>carol<TAB>carol@mail.net<TAB>viewer  
   104<TAB>dave<TAB>dave@site.org<TAB>editor  

 Your task:  
 1. Create a new file named /home/user/site/user_email.csv.  
 2. The new file must contain only the second and third columns (username and e-mail) taken from /home/user/site/accounts.tsv.  
 3. Convert the TAB delimiter to a single comma so that each line is formatted as:  

        username,email  

 4. Preserve the original order of the records.  
 5. The resulting /home/user/site/user_email.csv must end up with exactly these four lines and **nothing else** (no header, no trailing blank line):

        alice,alice@example.com
        bob,bob@domain.com
        carol,carol@mail.net
        dave,dave@site.org

 Important verification rules that the automated grader will apply:

 • /home/user/site/user_email.csv must exist and be world-readable (0644 or more open).  
 • The file must contain exactly 4 newline-terminated lines (Unix LF line-endings).  
 • Each line must contain one username, a single comma, and its matching e-mail address—spelling and case must match the original data precisely.  
 • No extra whitespace (spaces, tabs) is allowed anywhere in the new file.  
 • The original /home/user/site/accounts.tsv must remain unchanged.

 Use any standard shell utilities you like, but this can be accomplished in one straightforward command that combines column extraction and delimiter change.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

 1. Directory /home/user/site already exists and is writable by the current user.
 2. File /home/user/site/accounts.tsv exists with mode 0644 and exactly these four LF-terminated lines (written here with ▶ to visualise TAB characters):

    101▶alice▶alice@example.com▶admin
    102▶bob▶bob@domain.com▶editor
    103▶carol▶carol@mail.net▶viewer
    104▶dave▶dave@site.org▶editor

    (▶ represents the literal TAB byte 0x09.)

 Expected final state for successful completion:

 • A new file /home/user/site/user_email.csv exists with mode 0644.  
 • Its byte-exact contents (showing newline as ⏎) are:

    alice,alice@example.com⏎
    bob,bob@domain.com⏎
    carol,carol@mail.net⏎
    dave,dave@site.org⏎

 • No other files are modified or created inside /home/user/site.  
 • /home/user/site/accounts.tsv remains unchanged and still contains the original four TAB-separated lines.
